channel.title: About on AIGregate
channel.description: Recent content in About on AIGregate

item.title: April 19, 2026 tech news: Vercel Internal Systems Breached; Zero-Copy GPU Inference via Wasm; Trump Admin Coerced App Removals; Corporate Surveillance of Investigative Project; C++26 Standard Draft Finalized; Linux 7.0 Scheduler Regression; Nvidia CEO Defends China Chip Sales.
item.description:
Vercel Internal Systems Breached

Vercel has disclosed a security breach affecting a limited subset of customers. The company is urging users to rotate environment variables and utilize its sensitive variables feature to protect API keys, while reports link the intrusion to the threat group ShinyHunters.

  • https://decipher.sc/2026/04/19/vercel-says-internal-systems-hit-in-breach/
  • https://vercel.com/kb/bulletin/vercel-april-2026-security-incident

Zero-Copy GPU Inference via Wasm

A new implementation allows WebAssembly modules to share physical memory directly with the GPU on Apple Silicon, removing expensive data copies. This enables native-speed AI inference (e.g., Llama 3.2) within a sandboxed Wasm actor and allows for portable, serializable KV caches for stateful AI conversations.
